origin.name: Canada
origin.death_registration: Provincial vital statistics office (each province has its own)
origin.investigating_authority: Provincial coroner or medical examiner (varies by province)
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Unexpected, unattended, violent, or suspicious deaths. Province-specific coroner involvement.
origin.documentation_language: English throughout (French in Quebec, but English available).
origin.province_variation_note: Each of Canada's 10 provinces and 3 territories has its own vital statistics office and its own coroner or medical examiner system. Processing times vary.
origin.key_document: Province-issued death certificate (certified copy, in English)
origin.source: FCDO Canada travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Ottawa guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
special_rules: Documentation is in English throughout. No translation required.
Each province has its own vital statistics office and coroner. Processing times vary slightly.
Quebec issues death certificates in French, though English versions are available.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Canadian documentation is in English and typically accepted directly. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. British High Commission Ottawa and consulates in major cities can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Canada to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Canada. Repatriation to the UK takes 7-14 days. Provincial death registration and direct cargo flights explained. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
special_rules: Large Irish-Canadian diaspora. Well-established corridor. Aer Lingus operates direct Toronto and Montreal to Dublin routes.
Irish consulates in Toronto, Vancouver, and Ottawa handle consular matters.
Documentation in English. No translation required.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Canadian documentation in English and typically accepted directly. The Coroner is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. Irish consulates in Toronto, Ottawa, and Vancouver can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Canada to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Canada and needs to come home to Ireland. 7-14 days. Direct Aer Lingus flights and all documentation handled. Contact us 24/7.
